detaileddescription.para: symmetric 3x3 matrix Generated from class MR::SymMatrix3b. Supported MR_PassBy modes: MR_PassBy_DefaultConstruct, MR_PassBy_Copy, MR_PassBy_Move (and MR_PassBy_DefaultArgument and MR_PassBy_NoObject if supported by the callee).

symmetric 3x3 matrix Generated from class MR::SymMatrix3i. Supported MR_PassBy modes: MR_PassBy_DefaultConstruct, MR_PassBy_Copy, MR_PassBy_Move (and MR_PassBy_DefaultArgument and MR_PassBy_NoObject if supported by the callee).

symmetric 3x3 matrix Generated from class MR::SymMatrix3i64. Supported MR_PassBy modes: MR_PassBy_DefaultConstruct, MR_PassBy_Copy, MR_PassBy_Move (and MR_PassBy_DefaultArgument and MR_PassBy_NoObject if supported by the callee).

symmetric 3x3 matrix Generated from class MR::SymMatrix3f. Supported MR_PassBy modes: MR_PassBy_DefaultConstruct, MR_PassBy_Copy, MR_PassBy_Move (and MR_PassBy_DefaultArgument and MR_PassBy_NoObject if supported by the callee).

symmetric 3x3 matrix Generated from class MR::SymMatrix3d. Supported MR_PassBy modes: MR_PassBy_DefaultConstruct, MR_PassBy_Copy, MR_PassBy_Move (and MR_PassBy_DefaultArgument and MR_PassBy_NoObject if supported by the callee).

symmetric 3x3 matrix Generated from class MR::SymMatrix3<unsigned char>. Supported MR_PassBy modes: MR_PassBy_DefaultConstruct, MR_PassBy_Copy, MR_PassBy_Move (and MR_PassBy_DefaultArgument and MR_PassBy_NoObject if supported by the callee).

Constructs an empty (default-constructed) instance. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_SymMatrix3b_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

Constructs an array of empty (default-constructed) instances, of the specified size. Will never return null. The array must be destroyed using MR_SymMatrix3b_DestroyArray(). Use MR_SymMatrix3b_OffsetMutablePtr() and MR_SymMatrix3b_OffsetPtr() to access the array elements.

Offsets a pointer to an array element by i positions (not bytes). Use only if you're certain that the pointer points to an array element. The reference to the parameter ptr might be preserved in the return value.

Offsets a pointer to an array element by i positions (not bytes). Use only if you're certain that the pointer points to an array element. The reference to the parameter ptr might be preserved in the return value.

Generated from constructor MR::SymMatrix3b::SymMatrix3b. Parameter _other can not be null. It is a single object. The reference to things referred to by the parameter _other (if any) might be preserved in the constructed object. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_SymMatrix3b_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

Must call MR_SymMatrix3f_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

briefdescription.para: Destroys a heap-allocated instance of MR_SymMatrix3f. Does nothing if the pointer is null.
Destroys a heap-allocated array of MR_SymMatrix3f. Does nothing if the pointer is null.

detaileddescription.para: Generated from method MR::SymMatrix3f::operator=. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter _other can not be null. It is a single object. The reference to things referred to by the parameter _other (if any) might be preserved in this object. The returned pointer will never be null. It is non-owning, do NOT destroy it. When this function is called, this object will drop any object references it had previously.
Generated from method MR::SymMatrix3f::identity. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_SymMatrix3f_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.
Generated from method MR::SymMatrix3f::diagonal. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_SymMatrix3f_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.
computes trace of the matrix Generated from method MR::SymMatrix3f::trace. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object.
computes the squared norm of the matrix, which is equal to the sum of 9 squared elements Generated from method MR::SymMatrix3f::normSq. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object.
computes determinant of the matrix Generated from method MR::SymMatrix3f::det. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object.

computes inverse matrix Generated from method MR::SymMatrix3f::inverse. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_SymMatrix3f_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

computes inverse matrix given determinant of this Generated from method MR::SymMatrix3f::inverse. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_SymMatrix3f_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

Generated from method MR::SymMatrix3f::operator+=. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter b can not be null. It is a single object. The returned pointer will never be null. It is non-owning, do NOT destroy it.

Generated from method MR::SymMatrix3f::operator-=. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter b can not be null. It is a single object. The returned pointer will never be null. It is non-owning, do NOT destroy it.
Generated from method MR::SymMatrix3f::operator*=. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. The returned pointer will never be null. It is non-owning, do NOT destroy it.
Generated from method MR::SymMatrix3f::operator/=. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. The returned pointer will never be null. It is non-owning, do NOT destroy it.

returns eigenvalues of the matrix in ascending order (diagonal matrix L), and optionally returns corresponding unit eigenvectors in the rows of orthogonal matrix V, M*V^T = V^T*L; M = V^T*L*V Generated from method MR::SymMatrix3f::eigens. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter eigenvectors defaults to a null pointer in C++.
computes not-unit eigenvector corresponding to a not-repeating eigenvalue Generated from method MR::SymMatrix3f::eigenvector. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object.

for not-degenerate matrix returns just inverse matrix, otherwise returns degenerate matrix, which performs inversion on not-kernel subspace;
tol
relative epsilon-tolerance for too small number detection
rank
optional output for this matrix rank according to given tolerance
space
rank=1: unit direction of solution line, rank=2: unit normal to solution plane, rank=3: zero vector Generated from method MR::SymMatrix3f::pseudoinverse. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter tol has a default argument: std::numeric_limits<float>::epsilon(), pass a null pointer to use it. Parameter rank defaults to a null pointer in C++. Parameter space defaults to a null pointer in C++. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_SymMatrix3f_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

Constructs an empty (default-constructed) instance. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_SymMatrix3d_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

Constructs an array of empty (default-constructed) instances, of the specified size. Will never return null. The array must be destroyed using MR_SymMatrix3d_DestroyArray(). Use MR_SymMatrix3d_OffsetMutablePtr() and MR_SymMatrix3d_OffsetPtr() to access the array elements.

Offsets a pointer to an array element by i positions (not bytes). Use only if you're certain that the pointer points to an array element. The reference to the parameter ptr might be preserved in the return value.

Offsets a pointer to an array element by i positions (not bytes). Use only if you're certain that the pointer points to an array element. The reference to the parameter ptr might be preserved in the return value.

returns eigenvalues of the matrix in ascending order (diagonal matrix L), and optionally returns corresponding unit eigenvectors in the rows of orthogonal matrix V, M*V^T = V^T*L; M = V^T*L*V Generated from method MR::SymMatrix3d::eigens. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter eigenvectors defaults to a null pointer in C++.
computes not-unit eigenvector corresponding to a not-repeating eigenvalue Generated from method MR::SymMatrix3d::eigenvector. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object.

for not-degenerate matrix returns just inverse matrix, otherwise returns degenerate matrix, which performs inversion on not-kernel subspace;
tol
relative epsilon-tolerance for too small number detection
rank
optional output for this matrix rank according to given tolerance
space
rank=1: unit direction of solution line, rank=2: unit normal to solution plane, rank=3: zero vector Generated from method MR::SymMatrix3d::pseudoinverse. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter tol has a default argument: std::numeric_limits<double>::epsilon(), pass a null pointer to use it. Parameter rank defaults to a null pointer in C++. Parameter space defaults to a null pointer in C++. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_SymMatrix3d_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

Constructs an empty (default-constructed) instance. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_SymMatrix3_unsigned_char_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

Constructs an array of empty (default-constructed) instances, of the specified size. Will never return null. The array must be destroyed using MR_SymMatrix3_unsigned_char_DestroyArray(). Use MR_SymMatrix3_unsigned_char_OffsetMutablePtr() and MR_SymMatrix3_unsigned_char_OffsetPtr() to access the array elements.

Offsets a pointer to an array element by i positions (not bytes). Use only if you're certain that the pointer points to an array element. The reference to the parameter ptr might be preserved in the return value.

Offsets a pointer to an array element by i positions (not bytes). Use only if you're certain that the pointer points to an array element. The reference to the parameter ptr might be preserved in the return value.
